items.title: Suvendu Adhikari sworn in as CM. Will Bengal get a woman Deputy CM? What we know so far
items.summary: Looking at other states, the BJP has deputy chief ministers in its governments in Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan. The saffron party may want to have the same structure in Bengal as well.

items.title: Mamata Banerjee refused to resign as CM. Now Suvendu Adhikari takes oath — what happened in between?
items.summary: In the Indian Constitution, an outgoing Chief Minister typically resigns following an electoral defeat to allow the new government to be formed.

items.title: Why Amit Shah’s visit to Karnataka shows just how much BJP needs Yediyurappa
items.summary: A rally and the home minister in attendance—the BJP is celebrating the 83-year-old with full pomp to mark five decades in public life.

items.title: Suvendu Adhikari takes oath: From a TMC poster boy to BJP's first Bengal CM - a look at his political journey
items.summary: In the last five years, Suvendu Adhikari emerged as the BJP’s most formidable challenger to the TMC, delivering a crushing blow by defeating Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee and ultimately creating history as Bengal’s first BJP Chief Minister since Independence.

items.title: BJP's first govt in Bengal: PM Modi, senior ministers attend swearing-in ceremony — Who all are present?
items.summary: Thousands of BJP supporters from across the country and abroad also poured into the city for the first BJP government in West Bengal, turning Kolkata's iconic Brigade Parade Grounds into a sea of saffron.

items.title: West Bengal LIVE UPDATES: Suvendu Adhikari sworn in as Bengal’s first BJP CM
items.summary: Suvendu Adhikari, the leader of West Bengal's Legislative Party, is all set to take the oath of office as chief minister as the state moves into the BJP era of government for the first time.

items.title: Tamil Nadu LIVE UPDATES: TVK chief Vijay becomes chief minister, 9 other ministers take oath
items.summary: TVK chief takes oath along with 9 other TVK leaders who will be ministers in Vijay's maiden cabinet. Event attended by Rahul Gandhi, other allies. Stay tuned to ThePrint for LIVE UPDATES.

items.title: Amid Tamil Nadu govt formation drama, TVK says AMMK’s Kamaraj backed Vijay in letter, rebuts Dhinakaran’s claim
items.summary: Earlier, Dhinakaran claimed the letter supporting TVK is a case of ‘horse trading’ and forgery. He reaffirmed that AMMK's lone MLA-elect, Kamaraj S, remains loyal to the NDA and AIADMK. Dhinakaran officially backed AIADMK's Edappadi K Palaniswami (EPS) for the CM post.

items.title: Congress will defeat Modi, see what we do then, Rahul Gandhi says at Sadbhav Yatra in Gurugram
items.summary: Joining former MP Brijendra Singh’s march, Rahul Gandhi said that no other party could survive the BJP’s misuse of ECI, ED, CBI, and I-T department, but Congress’s ‘Babbar Shers’ were not afraid.

items.title: Vijay oath-taking confusion: Governor’s role, floor test rules and past Supreme Court judgment
items.summary: As per the Sarkaria Commission Report (1983), which discusses the role of the governor, if a single party has an absolute majority in the Assembly, the leader of that party should automatically be asked to become the Chief Minister.

items.title: Venugopal, Satheesan, Chennithala: Who will be Congress’ choice for Kerala CM? It’s complicated
items.summary: With 3 big leaders in contention, an intense behind-the-scenes battle has unfolded through internal consultations, lobbying by rival camps, street demonstrations. Decision will be announced by Sunday.

items.title: Suvendu Adhikari’s net worth: No car, no gold, just ₹12,000 cash declared by BJP’s first Bengal CM taking oath today
items.summary: Suvendu Adhikari’s net worth: In the election affidavit filed before contesting the West Bengal Election 2026, Suvendu Adhikari has declared a net worth of ₹85.87 lakh. This includes ₹24.57 lakh in movable assets and ₹61.30 lakh in immovable assets, as per the affidavit.

items.title: TN game of thrones: Cloud over Vijay oath-taking as IUML says it won’t back TVK, VCK still silent
items.summary: Meanwhile, AMMK chief TTV Dhinakaran said he has submitted a letter of support to Tamil Nadu Governor urging him to invite AIADMK's Palaniswami to form the government.

items.title: Suvendu Adhikari is West Bengal CM. The right-hand man who scripted Didi’s doom
items.summary: From being the power behind Mamata's 2011 victory to Trinamool rebel in 2020, Adhikari has ploughed his furrow.

items.title: ‘We don’t abandon the other in difficult times’—Akhilesh’s message after Congress dumps DMK
items.summary: In X post, SP chief posts images of himself with TMC supremo Mamata & DMK chief Stalin. Congress has offered conditional support to TVK to form govt in Tamil Nadu.

items.title: 5 times parties formed governments sans majority as Vijay's TVK scambles for numbers in Tamil Nadu
items.summary: While the Governor has sought proof of majority to form the government from Vijay, there have, however, been occasions when the party sans majority was asked to form a government.

items.title: Who will be BJP's first-ever Bengal CM? Amit Shah lands in Kolkata for crucial meeting with new MLAs
items.summary: Amit Shah was received at the Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose International Airport by BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari, among others. From the airport, he went to a hotel in New Town.
